analysis_result: Approximately 52-57% of the QC-passing nuclei in the premalignant atypical neurofibroma (NF110) snRNA-seq sample are immune cells. Primary (strict) estimate: 3,943 / 7,499 = 52.6% (PTPRC>=50% of cluster cells positive together with a myeloid/T-cell lineage marker). A looser cluster rule (PTPRC>=25% plus any lineage marker) yields 4,241 / 7,499 = 56.6%. A marker-only PTPRC>0 proxy gives 3,942 / 7,499 = 52.6%. These estimates converge on a majority (>50%) of the post-QC nuclei in NF110 being PTPRC+ immune cells, dominated by CD14+/CD163+/CSF1R+/ITGAX+ myeloid/macrophage clusters (1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 11) with a small CD3D/CD3E+ T-cell cluster (9).
denominator_used: N = 7,499 post-QC nuclei from the NF110 (atypical neurofibroma, GSM6285493) 10X matrix after filtering to >=500 detected genes and <10% mitochondrial UMIs. Raw droplets in the matrix = 6,794,880; cells passing >=500-gene filter = 7,500; after <10% mito = 7,499.
assumptions_made: NF110 (GSM6285493, strain 'NF', tissue 'Neurofibroma') is interpreted as the premalignant atypical/G3 neurofibroma sample (only NF sample in GSE207399; MPNSTs are labeled MPNST_G1/G2); QC thresholds >=500 genes and <10% mito as specified in the prompt (paper-reported thresholds were 1,500 genes and 15% mito, so this slightly increases cell yield and should be a superset of paper-retained cells); log-normalize (target 1e4) + HVG (seurat, top 3,000) + PCA (50 comps) + Leiden (resolution 0.8) with random_state=42; immune clusters identified when PTPRC>=50% positive cells together with >=25% cells expressing any of CD14/CD68/CD163/CSF1R/ITGAX (myeloid) or >=10% cells expressing CD3D/CD3E (T cells); borderline clusters (12, 13) with PTPRC 28-30% were excluded from the strict estimate; background/sparse expression inflates PTPRC proxy slightly but the three estimates converge.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- /data/GSE207399_10x/GSM6285493_NF110_{matrix.mtx.gz,features.tsv.gz,barcodes.tsv.gz} staged into one directory and loaded with scanpy.read_10x_mtx (var_names='gene_symbols').
- Raw shape: 6,794,880 droplets x 33,694 genes.
- Series matrix (GSE207399) confirms GSM6285493 = Tumor NF110, Sample_source_name = 'Neurofibroma', strain = 'NF' (the only non-MPNST sample in the series).

B. COHORT DEFINITION (post-QC nuclei in NF110)
- Filter 1: n_genes_by_counts >= 500 -> 7,500 cells
- Filter 2: pct_counts_mt < 10% -> 7,499 cells
- Filter 3: filter_genes(min_cells=3) -> 25,441 genes x 7,499 cells
- TOTAL = 7,499 post-QC nuclei (denominator).

C. VARIABLE CODING / IMMUNE CALL
- Log-normalize (normalize_total target_sum=1e4, log1p) + HVG seurat (n=3,000) + PCA (50 comps) + Leiden resolution=0.8, random_state=42.
- 18 Leiden clusters (0-17). Sizes: 0:1381, 1:1334, 2:693, 3:659, 4:633, 5:452, 6:417, 7:345, 8:328, 9:291, 10:242, 11:169, 12:168, 13:130, 14:93, 15:90, 16:60, 17:14.
- Assessed immune lineage markers present in gene symbols: PTPRC (CD45), CD3D/E/G, CD2, CD4, CD8A, CD14, CD68, CD163, LYZ, CSF1R, ITGAM, ITGAX, AIF1, CD19, MS4A1.
- Per-cluster fraction of cells with expression >0 (selected):
  cluster PTPRC CD14 CD68 CD163 CD3D CD3E LYZ CSF1R AIF1 ITGAX
  1 .906 .430 .136 .699 .002 .000 .124 .642 .151 .533
  2 .915 .456 .128 .580 .003 .001 .032 .633 .100 .609
  3 .863 .457 .109 .813 .000 .002 .065 .542 .096 .492
  5 .918 .378 .122 .595 .000 .004 .137 .624 .142 .628
  7 .586 .220 .064 .397 .000 .000 .032 .383 .055 .270
  9 .990 .127 .021 .096 .134 .186 .021 .089 .038 .141
  11 .746 .420 .148 .509 .024 .030 .089 .485 .130 .462
  Non-immune clusters (0, 4, 6, 8, 10, 14, 15) have PTPRC fractions 0.05-0.18 and macrophage/T-cell markers <0.10.
- Immune cluster rule (strict, primary): PTPRC_frac >= 0.5 AND (max(CD14,CD68,CD163,CSF1R,ITGAX) >= 0.25 OR max(CD3D,CD3E) >= 0.1). Matched clusters: 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 (total 3,943 cells).
- Immune cluster rule (lenient): PTPRC_frac >= 0.25 AND any lineage marker >= 0.15, OR PTPRC_frac >= 0.4. Matched: above plus 12, 13 (total 4,241 cells).
- Marker-only backup: PTPRC expression > 0 -> 3,942 cells.

D. COMPUTATION
- Strict cluster-based: immune = 3,943 / 7,499 = 0.5258 (52.58%).
- Lenient cluster-based: immune = 4,241 / 7,499 = 0.5655 (56.55%).
- PTPRC+ proxy: 3,942 / 7,499 = 0.5257 (52.57%).

E. RESULT
- In NF110 (premalignant atypical neurofibroma), immune cells comprise ~52-57% of post-QC nuclei, centered around 53%. The immune compartment is dominated by myeloid/macrophage populations (clusters 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 11: CD14+/CD163+/CSF1R+/ITGAX+) with a smaller T-cell cluster (9: CD3D+/CD3E+/CD8-implied by high PTPRC and presence of some CD3). This is strikingly high for a benign neurofibroma and is consistent with the known prominent Schwann-cell/macrophage niche of plexiform/atypical NFs.

F. CAVEATS
- Only one atypical-NF snRNA-seq sample (NF110) exists in the dataset, so the 'proportion' is a single-sample value; no inter-sample variance.
- Paper QC thresholds were 1,500 genes / 15% mito; the prompt specified 500 genes / 10% mito which retains a slightly different cell set. Re-running with paper thresholds would likely give similar relative proportions but different absolute N.
- Cluster assignments depend on Leiden resolution (0.8) and PC depth; different resolutions may split/merge the borderline clusters 12 and 13, changing the lenient estimate by a few percentage points.
- snRNA-seq nuclei counts do not perfectly mirror bulk tissue cell composition; droplet recovery biases and nuclear isolation may preferentially recover certain lineages (e.g. macrophages often recover well from nuclei preps).
- Marker-based identification is probabilistic; some background PTPRC expression exists in non-immune clusters (5-15%), which is typical of ambient RNA.
